documentation: Creates all joints given a config.

classes.name: brax.v1.physics.spring_joints.Joint

methods.name: brax.v1.physics.spring_joints.Joint.__init__
documentation:
Creates a Joint that connects two bodies and constrains their movement.

Args:
  joints: list of joints (all of the same type) to batch together
  body: batched body that contain the parents and children of each joint
  spring_damping_coeff: coefficient for setting default spring damping

methods.name: brax.v1.physics.spring_joints.Joint.apply
documentation:
Returns impulses to constrain and align bodies connected by a joint.

Only called by springy joints.

Args:
  qp: State data for system

Returns:
  dP: Impulses on all bodies to maintain joint constraints

A spring joint connects two bodies and constrains their movement.

This constraint is determined by axes that define how to bodies may move in
relation to one-another.

classes.name: brax.v1.physics.spring_joints.Revolute

methods.name: brax.v1.physics.spring_joints.Revolute.apply_reduced
documentation: Returns calculated impulses in compressed joint space.

methods.name: brax.v1.physics.spring_joints.Revolute.axis_angle
documentation: Returns axes and angles of a single joint.

A springy revolute joint constrains two bodies around a single axis.

Constructs a revolute joint where the parent's local x-axis is constrained
to point in the same direction as the child's local x-axis.  This construction
follows the line of nodes convention shared by the universal and spherical
joints for x-y'-z'' intrinsic euler angles.

classes.name: brax.v1.physics.spring_joints.Universal

methods.name: brax.v1.physics.spring_joints.Universal.apply_reduced
documentation: Returns calculated impulses in compressed joint space.

methods.name: brax.v1.physics.spring_joints.Universal.axis_angle
documentation: Returns axes and angles of a single joint.

A springy universal joint constrains two bodies around two axes.

Constructs a universal joint defined as the first two degrees of freedom
of a spherical joint.  See `SpringSpherical` for details.

classes.name: brax.v1.physics.spring_joints.Spherical

methods.name: brax.v1.physics.spring_joints.Spherical.apply_reduced
documentation: Returns calculated impulses in compressed joint space.

methods.name: brax.v1.physics.spring_joints.Spherical.axis_angle
documentation: Returns axes and angles of a single joint.

A springy spherical joint constrains two bodies around three axes.

Constructs a spherical joint which returns intrinsic euler angles in the
  x-y'-z'' convention between the parent and child.  Uses the line of nodes
  construction described in section 3.2.3.2 here:
  https://www.sedris.org/wg8home/Documents/WG80485.pdf

functions.name: brax.v1.jumpy.safe_norm
documentation:
Calculates a linalg.norm(x) that's safe for gradients at x=0.

Avoids a poorly defined gradient for jnp.linal.norm(0) see
https://github.com/google/jax/issues/3058 for details
Args:
  x: A jnp.array
  axis: The axis along which to compute the norm

Returns:
  Norm of the array x.

classes.name: brax.v1.physics.integrators.Euler

methods.name: brax.v1.physics.integrators.Euler.__init__
documentation:
Creates an integrator.

Args:
  config: brax config

methods.name: brax.v1.physics.integrators.Euler.kinetic
documentation:
Performs a kinetic integration step.

Args:
  qp: State data to be integrated

Returns:
  State data advanced by one kinematic integration step.

methods.name: brax.v1.physics.integrators.Euler.update
documentation:
Performs an arg dependent integrator step.

Args:
  qp: State data to be integrated
  acc_p: Acceleration level updates to apply to qp
  vel_p: Velocity level updates to apply to qp
  pos_q: Position level updates to apply to qp

Returns:
  State data advanced by one potential integration step.

methods.name: brax.v1.physics.integrators.Euler.velocity_projection
documentation:
Performs the position based dynamics velocity projection step.

The velocity and angular velocity must respect the spatial and quaternion
distance (respectively) between qp and qpold.

Args:
  qp: The current qp
  qp_prev: The qp at the previous timestep

Returns:
  qp with velocities pinned to respect the distance traveled since qpold
Base integrator class.

documentation: Creates all joints given a config.

classes.name: brax.v1.physics.joints.Joint

methods.name: brax.v1.physics.joints.Joint.__init__
documentation:
Creates a Joint that connects two bodies and constrains their movement.

Args:
  joints: list of joints (all of the same type) to batch together
  body: batched body that contain the parents and children of each joint
  solver_scale_pos: Magnitude of jacobi update for position based updates
  solver_scale_ang: Magnitude of jacobi update for angular position based
    update
  free_dofs: Number of free (not-fixed) degrees of freedom per joint

methods.name: brax.v1.physics.joints.Joint.apply
documentation:
Returns position-based update to constrain bodies connected by a joint.

Args:
  qp: State data for system

Returns:
  dQ: Change in position and quaternions to enforce joint constraint.

methods.name: brax.v1.physics.joints.Joint.damp
documentation:
Returns an acceleration-level damping term for the joint.

Args:
  qp: State data for system

Returns:
  dP: Change in velocity from joint damping.
TODO: replace this function call with a more efficient version

methods.name: brax.v1.physics.joints.Joint.apply_angle_update
documentation: Calculates a position based angular update.

methods.name: brax.v1.physics.joints.Joint.apply_position_update
documentation:
Calculates a position based positional update.

Args:
  qp_p: First body participating in update
  qp_c: Second body participating in update:
  pos_p: World space location on first body
  pos_c: World space location on second body
Returns: A position based update that adjusts qp_p and qp_c so that pos_p
  and pos_c are the same in world space.

methods.name: brax.v1.physics.joints.Joint.angle_vel
documentation:
Returns joint angle and velocity.

Args:
  qp: State data for system

Returns:
  angle: n-tuple of joint angles where n = # DoF of the joint
  vel: n-tuple of joint velocities where n = # DoF of the joint

methods.name: brax.v1.physics.joints.Joint.apply_reduced
documentation:
Returns impulses to constrain and align bodies connected by a joint.

Operates in reduced joint space.

Args:
  qp_p: Joint parent state data
  qp_c: Joint child state data

Returns:
  dp_p: Joint parent impulse
  dp_c: Joint child impulse

methods.name: brax.v1.physics.joints.Joint.axis_angle
documentation:
Returns axes and angles of a single joint.

vmap across axis_angle to get all joints.

Args:
  qp_p: State for parent body
  qp_c: State for child body

Returns:
  axis: n-tuple of joint axes where n = # DoF of the joint
  angle: n-tuple of joint angles where n = # DoF of the joint

A joint connects two bodies and constrains their movement.

This constraint is determined by axes that define how to bodies may move in
relation to one-another.

classes.name: brax.v1.physics.joints.Revolute

methods.name: brax.v1.physics.joints.Revolute.apply_reduced
documentation: Constructs the position based constraint update for a parent and child.

methods.name: brax.v1.physics.joints.Revolute.axis_angle
documentation: Returns axes and angles of a single joint.

A revolute joint constrains two bodies around a single axis.

Constructs a revolute joint where the parent's local x-axis is constrained
to point in the same direction as the child's local x-axis.  This construction
follows the line of nodes convention shared by the universal and spherical
joints for x-y'-z'' intrinsic euler angles.

classes.name: brax.v1.physics.joints.Spherical

methods.name: brax.v1.physics.joints.Spherical.axis_angle
documentation: Returns axes and angles of a single joint.

A spherical joint constrains two bodies around three axes.

Constructs a spherical joint which returns intrinsic euler angles in the
  x-y'-z'' convention between the parent and child.  Uses the line of nodes
  construction described in section 3.2.3.2 here:
  https://www.sedris.org/wg8home/Documents/WG80485.pdf

documentation:
Returns world information about a point relative to a part.

methods.name: brax.v1.physics.base.QP.world_velocity
documentation:
Returns the velocity of the point on a rigidbody in world space.

Args:

A coordinate and time derivative frame for a brax body.

  rot: Rotation about center of mass, represented as a quaternion.
  vel: Velocity.
  ang: Angular velocity about center of mass.

classes.name: brax.v1.physics.base.Info
documentation:
Auxilliary data calculated during the dynamics of each physics step.

Attributes:
  contact: External contact forces applied at a step
  joint: Joint constraint forces applied at a step
  actuator: Actuator forces applied at a step
  contact_pos: Contact point position in world coordinates.
  contact_normal: Contact normal.
  contact_penetration: Contact penetration.

  impulse: Impulse vector
  pos: Location of the impulse relative to the body's center of mass

A body is a solid, non-deformable object with some mass and shape.

  mass: Mass of the body.

Rotates a vector vec by a unit quaternion quat.

Args:

  quat: (4,) a quaternion

Returns:
  ndarray(3) containing vec rotated by quat.

functions.name: brax.v1.math.inv_rotate
documentation:
Rotates a vector by the inverse of a unit quaternion.

Args:
  vec: (3,) a vector
  quat: (4,) a quaternion

Returns:
  A vector rotated by quat^{-1}

functions.name: brax.v1.math.ang_to_quat
documentation:
Converts angular velocity to a quaternion.

Args:
  ang: (3,) angular velocity

Returns:
  A rotation quaternion.

functions.name: brax.v1.math.euler_to_quat
documentation: Converts euler rotations in degrees to quaternion.

functions.name: brax.v1.math.quat_to_euler

Returns the axis-angle representation of a quaternion.

Args:
  q: (4,) a quaternion

Returns:

functions.name: brax.v1.math.signed_angle
documentation:
Calculates the signed angle between two vectors along an axis.

Args:
  axis: (3,) common axis around which to calculate change in angle
  ref_p: (3,) vector pointing at 0-degrees offset in the parent's frame
  ref_c: (3,) vector pointing at 0-degrees offset in the child's frame

Returns:
  The signed angle between two parts.

functions.name: brax.v1.math.quat_mul
documentation:
Multiplies two quaternions.

Args:
  u: (4,) quaternion (w,x,y,z)
  v: (4,) quaternion (w,x,y,z)

Returns:
  A quaternion u * v.

functions.name: brax.v1.math.vec_quat_mul
documentation:
Multiplies a vector and a quaternion.

This is a convenience method for multiplying two quaternions when
one of the quaternions has a 0-value w-part, i.e.:
quat_mul([0.,a,b,c], [d,e,f,g])

It is slightly more efficient than constructing a 0-w-part quaternion
from the vector.

Args:
  u: (3,) vector representation of the quaternion (0.,x,y,z)
  v: (4,) quaternion (w,x,y,z)

Returns:
  A quaternion u * v.

functions.name: brax.v1.math.quat_rot_axis
documentation:
Provides a quaternion that describes rotating around axis v by angle.

Args:
  axis: (3,) axis (x,y,z)
  angle: () float angle to rotate by

Returns:
  A quaternion that rotates around v by angle

functions.name: brax.v1.math.quat_inv
documentation:
Calculates the inverse of quaternion q.

Args:
  q: (4,) quaternion [w, x, y, z]

Returns:
  The inverse of q, where qmult(q, inv_quat(q)) = [1, 0, 0, 0].

functions.name: brax.v1.math.relative_quat
documentation: Returns the relative quaternion from q1 to q2.

methods.name: brax.v1.physics.colliders.Collider.position_apply
documentation:
Returns a position based update that resolves a collisions for QP.

  qp_prev: State data before the most recent kinetic update.

functions.name: brax.v1.physics.geometry.clip
documentation:
Clips a subject polygon against a clipping polygon.

A parallelized clipping algorithm for convex polygons. The result is a set of
vertices on the clipped subject polygon in the subject polygon plane.

Args:
  clipping_poly: The polygon that we use to clip the subject polygon against.
  subject_poly: The polygon that gets clipped.
  clipping_normal: Normal of the clipping polygon.
  subject_normal: Normal of the subject polygon.

Returns:
  clipped_pts: The clipped polygon.
  mask: True if a point is in the clipping polygon.

functions.name: brax.v1.physics.geometry._create_sat_edge_contact
documentation: Creates an edge contact from a separating axis.

functions.name: brax.v1.physics.geometry._create_sat_contact_manifold
documentation:
Creates a contact manifold between two convex polygons.

The polygon faces are expected to have a clockwise winding order so that
clipping plane normals point away from the polygon center.

Args:
  clipping_poly: The reference polygon to clip the contact against.
  subject_poly: The subject polygon to clip contacts onto.
  clipping_norm: The clipping polygon normal.
  subject_norm: The subject polygon normal.
  sep_axis_sign: The sign of the separating axis wrt the clipping polygon
    normal.

Returns:
  contact: Contact object.

functions.name: brax.v1.physics.geometry.sat_hull_hull
documentation:
Runs the Separating Axis Test for a pair of hulls.

Given two convex hulls, the Separating Axis Test finds a separating axis
between all edge pairs and face pairs. Edge pairs create a single contact
point and face pairs create a contact manifold (up to four contact points).
We return both the edge and face contacts. Valid contacts can be checked with
penetration > 0. Resulting edge contacts should be preferred over face
contacts.

Args:
  faces_a: An ndarray of hull A's polygon faces.
  faces_b: An ndarray of hull B's polygon faces.
  vertices_a: Vertices for hull A.
  vertices_b: Vertices for hull B.
  normals_a: Normal vectors for hull A's polygon faces.
  normals_b: Normal vectors for hull B's polygon faces.

Returns:
  edge_contact: An edge contact.
  face_contact: A face contact.

functions.name: brax.v1.physics.geometry.rotate_point_axis
documentation:
Convenience function for rotating qps in world space around a point.

For a selection of body indices, this function takes those bodies and
rotates them around some point, around an axis emanating from that point.

Args:
  qp: (n, ...) Full state qp for system
  rotate_idxs: (m<=n,) integer body idxs to be rotated
  point: (3,) a point in space to rotate about
  axis: (3,) an axis in space to rotate around
  angle: float angle to rotate by

Returns:
  Full state qp with bodies at rotate_idxs rotated.

methods.name: brax.base.Base.tree_replace
documentation:
Creates a new object with parameters set.

Args:
  params: a dictionary of key value pairs to replace

Returns:
  data clas with new values

Example:
  If a system has 3 links, the following code replaces the mass
  of each link in the System:
  >>> sys = sys.tree_replace(
  >>>     {'link.inertia.mass', jp.array([1.0, 1.2, 1.3])})

Base functionality extending all brax types.

These methods allow for brax types to be operated like arrays/matrices.

classes.name: brax.base.Link
documentation:
A rigid segment of an articulated body.

Links are connected to each other by joints.  By moving (rotating or
translating) the joints, the entire system can be articulated.

Attributes:
  transform: transform for the link frame relative to the parent frame
  joint: transform for the joint frame relative to the link frame
  inertia: mass, center of mass location, and inertia of this link
  invweight: mean inverse inertia at init_q
  constraint_stiffness: (num_link,) constraint spring for joint.
  constraint_vel_damping: (num_link,) linear damping for constraint spring.
  constraint_limit_stiffness: (num_link,) constraint for angle limits
  constraint_ang_damping: (num_link,) angular damping for constraint spring.

classes.name: brax.base.DoF
documentation:
A degree of freedom in the system.

Attributes:
  motion: spatial motion (linear or angular) of this DoF
  armature: models the inertia of a rotor (moving part of a motor)
  stiffness: restorative force back to zero position
  damping: restorative force back to zero velocity
  limit: tuple of min, max angle limits
  invweight: diagonal inverse inertia at init_qpos
  solver_params: (7,) limit constraint solver parameters

classes.name: brax.base.Contact
documentation:
Contact between two geometries.

Attributes:

  elasticity: bounce/restitution encountered when hitting another geometry

classes.name: brax.base.Actuator
documentation:
Actuator, transforms an input signal into a force (motor or thruster).

Attributes:
  q_id: (num_actuators,) q index associated with an actuator
  qd_id: (num_actuators,) qd index associated with an actuator
  ctrl_range: (num_actuators, 2) actuator control range
  force_range: (num_actuators, 2) actuator force range
  gain: (num_actuators,) scaling factor for each actuator control input
  gear: (num_actuators,) scaling factor for each actuator force output
  bias_q: (num_actuators,) bias applied by q (e.g. position actuators)
  bias_qd: (num_actuators,) bias applied by qd (e.g. velocity actuators)

documentation: Returns the number of links in the system.

documentation: Returns the link index corresponding to each system dof.

methods.name: brax.base.System.act_size
documentation: Returns the act dimension for the system.

Describes a physical environment: its links, joints and geometries.

Attributes:
  gravity: (3,) linear universal force applied during forward dynamics
  viscosity: (1,) viscosity of the medium applied to all links
  density: (1,) density of the medium applied to all links
  link: (num_link,) the links in the system
  dof: (qd_size,) every degree of freedom for the system
  actuator: actuators that can be applied to links
  init_q: (q_size,) initial q position for the system
  elasticity: bounce/restitution encountered when hitting another geometry
  vel_damping: (1,) linear vel damping applied to each body.
  ang_damping: (1,) angular vel damping applied to each body.
  baumgarte_erp: how aggressively interpenetrating bodies should push away\
              from one another
  spring_mass_scale: a float that scales mass as `mass^(1 - x)`
  spring_inertia_scale: a float that scales inertia diag as `inertia^(1 - x)`
  joint_scale_ang: scale for position-based joint rotation update
  joint_scale_pos: scale for position-based joint position update
  collide_scale: fraction of position based collide update to apply
  enable_fluid: (1,) enables or disables fluid forces based on the
    default viscosity and density parameters provided in the XML
  link_names: (num_link,) link names
  link_types: (num_link,) string specifying the joint type of each link
              valid types are:
              * 'f': free, full 6 dof (position + rotation), no parent link
              * '1': revolute,  1 dof, like a hinge
              * '2': universal, 2 dof, like a drive shaft joint
              * '3': spherical, 3 dof, like a ball joint
  link_parents: (num_link,) int list specifying the index of each link's
                parent link, or -1 if the link has no parent
  matrix_inv_iterations: maximum number of iterations of the matrix inverse
  solver_iterations: maximum number of iterations of the constraint solver
  solver_maxls: maximum number of line searches of the constraint solver
  mj_model: mujoco.MjModel that was used to build this brax System

functions.name: brax.kinematics.forward
documentation:
Converts joint position/velocity to transform/motion in world frame.

Args:
  sys: system defining the kinematic tree and other properties
  q: joint position vector
  qd: joint velocity vector

Returns:
  x: transform in world frame
  xd: motion in world frame

functions.name: brax.kinematics.world_to_joint
documentation: Moves into the joint frame of a maximal coordinates kinematic tree.

functions.name: brax.kinematics.link_to_joint_frame
documentation:
Calculates 3-dof frames for joints corresponding to a given link motion.

Args:
  motion: Motion dof with leaves of shape (QD_WIDTH, 3)

Returns:
  Frame with all three dofs for this joint, and the handedness of the joint

Joint axes are not always aligned along the local frame of a link. For
example, a joint might be along the local z-axis, and we would need to
find the rotation that takes local-x to local-z, or:
from_to([1., 0., 0.], [0., 0., 1.]).

For joints with more than 1 axis (e.g., universal, spherical), there's one
additional degree of freedom in the orientation of the second axis, which is
set by performing one final rotation of the link-frame y-axis so that it
aligns with the joint-frame y-axis.

Combined, these two rotations move the identity link-frame into the joint
frame, where the new local-x and local-y point along the directions specified
in the system dof.

We also need translational components because the prismatic components of a
joint might not be aligned with the rotational components of the joint.

functions.name: brax.kinematics.axis_angle_ang
documentation:
Returns axes, torque axes, angles, and angular velocities of a joint.

  This function calculates intrinsic euler angles in the x-y'-z'' convention
  between the parent and child.  It uses the line of nodes construction
  described in section 3.2.3.2 here:
  https://www.sedris.org/wg8home/Documents/WG80485.pdf

Args:
  j: The transform for this joint.
  joint_motion: motion degrees of freedom for this joint
  parity: handedness of this joint

Returns:
  Joint frame axis, angles, and auxiliary axes

functions.name: brax.kinematics.axis_slide_vel
documentation:
Returns axes and slide dofs for a joint.

Args:
  x: (3,) The transform for this joint type.
  xd: (3,) The motion for this joint type.
  motion: Motion degrees of freedom for this joint

Returns:
  Joint frame axis, positions, and velocities.

functions.name: brax.kinematics.inverse
documentation: Translates maximal coordinates into reduced coordinates.

pylint:disable=g-multiple-import

functions.name: brax.scan._take
documentation:
Takes idxs on any pytree given to it.

XLA executes x[jp.array([1, 2, 3])] slower than x[1:4], so we detect when
take indices are contiguous, and convert them to slices.

Args:
  obj: an input pytree
  idxs: indices to take

Returns:
  obj pytree with leaves taken by idxs

functions.name: brax.scan.tree
documentation:
Scan a function over each level of a system while carrying along state.

f is called once for each level of a system tree. running `tree` on tall tree
layouts (like long kinematic chains/ropes) may produce long unrolls that are
slow to jit.

Args:

: sys: a brax system defining the scan order
  f: a function to be scanned with the following type signature:

  > > def f(y, args) -> y
  >
  > where
  >
  > : `y` is the carry value and return value, in link parent order
  >   `*args` are input arguments with types matching `in_types`

  in_types: string specifying the type of each input arg:

  : 'l' is an input to be split according to link ranges
    'q' is an input to be split according to q ranges
    'd' is an input to be split according to qd ranges

  args: the input arguments corresponding to `in_types`
  reverse: if True, scans up the system tree from leaves to root, otherwise

  > root to leaves

Returns:

: The stacked outputs of `f`, matching the system link order.

functions.name: brax.scan.link_types
documentation:
Scan a function over System link type ranges.

Args:

: sys: system defining the kinematic tree and other properties
  f: a function to be scanned with the following type signature:

  > > def f(typ, args) -> y
  >
  > where
  >
  > : `typ` is the actuator, link type string
  >   `*args` are input arguments with types matching `in_types`
  >   `y` is an output arguments with types matching `out_type`

  in_types: string specifying the type of each input arg:

  : 'l' is an input to be split according to link ranges
    'q' is an input to be split according to q ranges
    'd' is an input to be split according to qd ranges

  out_types: string specifying the types of the outputs
  args: the input arguments corresponding to `in_types`

Returns:

: The stacked outputs of `f` matching the system link order.

functions.name: brax.math.rotate
documentation:
Rotates a vector vec by a unit quaternion quat.

Args:
  vec: (3,) a vector
  quat: (4,) a quaternion

Returns:
  ndarray(3) containing vec rotated by quat.

functions.name: brax.math.inv_rotate
documentation:
Rotates a vector vec by an inverted unit quaternion quat.

Args:
  vec: (3,) a vector
  quat: (4,) a quaternion

Returns:
  ndarray(3) containing vec rotated by the inverse of quat.

functions.name: brax.math.rotate_np
documentation:
Rotates a vector vec by a unit quaternion quat.

Args:
  vec: (3,) a vector
  quat: (4,) a quaternion

Returns:
  ndarray(3) containing vec rotated by quat.

functions.name: brax.math.ang_to_quat
documentation:
Converts angular velocity to a quaternion.

Args:
  ang: (3,) angular velocity

Returns:
  A rotation quaternion.

functions.name: brax.math.quat_mul
documentation:
Multiplies two quaternions.

Args:
  u: (4,) quaternion (w,x,y,z)
  v: (4,) quaternion (w,x,y,z)

Returns:
  A quaternion u * v.

functions.name: brax.math.quat_mul_np
documentation:
Multiplies two quaternions.

Args:
  u: (4,) quaternion (w,x,y,z)
  v: (4,) quaternion (w,x,y,z)

Returns:
  A quaternion u * v.

functions.name: brax.math.quat_inv
documentation:
Calculates the inverse of quaternion q.

Args:
  q: (4,) quaternion [w, x, y, z]

Returns:
  The inverse of q, where qmult(q, inv_quat(q)) = [1, 0, 0, 0].

functions.name: brax.math.quat_rot_axis
documentation:
Provides a quaternion that describes rotating around axis v by angle.

Args:
  axis: (3,) axis (x,y,z)
  angle: () float angle to rotate by

Returns:
  A quaternion that rotates around v by angle

functions.name: brax.math.quat_to_3x3
documentation: Converts quaternion to 3x3 rotation matrix.

functions.name: brax.math.quat_mul_ang
documentation: Multiplies a quat by an angular velocity.

functions.name: brax.math.signed_angle
documentation:
Calculates the signed angle between two vectors along an axis.

Args:
  axis: (3,) common axis around which to calculate change in angle
  ref_p: (3,) vector pointing at 0-degrees offset in the parent's frame
  ref_c: (3,) vector pointing at 0-degrees offset in the child's frame

Returns:
  The signed angle between two parts.

functions.name: brax.math.safe_arccos
documentation: Trigonometric inverse cosine, element-wise with safety clipping in grad.

functions.name: brax.math.safe_arcsin
documentation: Trigonometric inverse sine, element-wise with safety clipping in grad.

functions.name: brax.math.inv_3x3
documentation: Inverse specialized for 3x3 matrices.

functions.name: brax.math.orthogonals
documentation: Returns orthogonal vectors `b` and `c`, given a normal vector `a`.

functions.name: brax.math.solve_pgs
documentation: Projected Gauss-Seidel solver for a MLCP defined by matrix A and vector b.

functions.name: brax.math.inv_approximate
documentation:
Use Newton-Schulz iteration to solve `A^-1`.

Args:

: a: 2D array to invert
  a_inv: approximate solution to A^-1
  num_iter: number of iterations

Returns:

: A^-1 inverted matrix

functions.name: brax.math.safe_norm
documentation:
Calculates a linalg.norm(x) that's safe for gradients at x=0.

Avoids a poorly defined gradient for jnp.linal.norm(0) see
https://github.com/google/jax/issues/3058 for details
Args:
  x: A jnp.array
  axis: The axis along which to compute the norm

Returns:
  Norm of the array x.

functions.name: brax.math.normalize
documentation:
Normalizes an array.

Args:
  x: A jnp.array
  axis: The axis along which to compute the norm

Returns:
  A tuple of (normalized array x, the norm).

functions.name: brax.math.from_to
documentation: Calculates the quaternion that rotates unit vector v1 to unit vector v2.

functions.name: brax.math.euler_to_quat
documentation: Converts euler rotations in degrees to quaternion.

functions.name: brax.math.quat_to_euler
documentation: Converts quaternions to euler rotations in radians.

functions.name: brax.math.vec_quat_mul
documentation:
Multiplies a vector u and a quaternion v.

This is a convenience method for multiplying two quaternions when
one of the quaternions has a 0-value w-part, i.e.:
quat_mul([0.,a,b,c], [d,e,f,g])

It is slightly more efficient than constructing a 0-w-part quaternion
from the vector.

Args:
  u: (3,) vector representation of the quaternion (0.,x,y,z)
  v: (4,) quaternion (w,x,y,z)

Returns:
  A quaternion u * v.

functions.name: brax.math.relative_quat
documentation: Returns the relative quaternion from q1 to q2.
